anchored_threads.thread_name: The sthita-prajña / one whose insight is steady (verses 2.54-2.72)
anchored_threads.anchor_evidence: Indirect anchoring on the chapter's pedagogy: 2.10 is the structural pivot before Krishna begins teaching — and Krishna IS the chapter's exemplar of the steady mind. The engineering layer's preservation of 'smiling as if not amused, but settled' captures the chapter's later sthita-prajña vocabulary in narrative form: the settled state is the byproduct of an actual underlying state, not a technique. This anchors on the chapter's own claim that 'the steady mind is a real state, not an aspiration.'

progression_issue.explanation: Position 10 is the last verse of the chapter's pre-teaching scaffolding. The engineering layer correctly stays in the marker-of-pivot register and does not preempt 2.11's first-principles teaching.

verdict_payload.verdict_reason: BG 2.10 is the chapter's sanctioned pivot moment — Krishna about to speak. The chapter's thesis explicitly names the steady-mind / sthita-prajña as a real state (not an aspiration), and the engineering layer's preservation of 'state-not-technique' for the smile is doctrinally consistent with that hard edge. The honest LOW + STRETCHED tagging matches the chapter's sanctioned form for narrative verses. No hard edges violated, no counter-anti-thesis drift. PASS at the chapter's correct standard for narrative verses.

verdict_payload.satire_attempt: Local CTO Smiles Slightly Before Speaking, Single-Handedly Resolving Eight-Week Migration Debate. Sources confirm the smile was 'not amused but settled,' a phrase the management training company plans to license for the upcoming 'Krishna Smile Workshop' ($2,400/seat). Industry observers note this is the same body language that has historically resolved Mahabharata-scale conflicts.
verdict_payload.verdict_reason: The satire only lands if you ignore the verse's explicit refusal of the read: 'A reader extracts a leadership tactic from this verse — always smile before delivering hard feedback — they have over-read it.' The counter-example explicitly disqualifies the workshop satire: 'A leader who performs a smile to manipulate the room is not enacting Krishna in 2.10; they are performing affect.' To mock the verse you have to disagree with the meta-claim that affect-without-state is inversion, which is a real argument. PASS.

The single disagreement entry on prahasann iva names the doctrinally substantive axis precisely: both translators drop the 'iva' (as if) for English readability, but the particle is doctrinally significant — 'it qualifies the smile, distancing it from amusement. Krishna is not smiling AT Arjuna; he is smiling in the way a settled teacher smiles when a long-deferred conversation is finally ready to begin.' This identifies the philological feature (the iva particle), the translator pattern (dropping it for readability), and the doctrinal stake (the smile is not amused; it is settled-teacher-readiness, which the commentarial tradition reads carefully). One tight line of gold-standard explanation quality. Score 9, PASS.

Tags on BG 2.10 are team-state, team-culture. The team-state tag is shared with 2.9 (also tagged team-state) and the doctrines are coherent — both verses describe the room-state at a structural pivot, neither claims a leadership tactic from the state. The team-culture tag is shared with 2.7 (where 'team-culture' refers to the request-form discipline as a culture-level practice). The 2.7 and 2.10 use of 'team-culture' is coherent: 2.7 names the request-formulation discipline, 2.10 names the byproduct-vs-technique distinction — both are culture-level practices about how teams handle the structurally-difficult moment. No tag drift, no contradictions. No cross_references[] declared. Score 7 — coherent within visible scope; corpus-completion will be authoritative. PASS.

verse_specific_anchors: prahasann iva — smiling as-if; the analog explicitly preserves the iva-qualifier ('not amused, but settled')
senayor ubhayor madhye — between the two armies; the analog preserves this as 'the two armies of the room'
the structural-pivot role: the explicit identification that the verse marks the moment Krishna is about to begin teaching at 2.11
verdict_payload.verdict_reason: I tested against three candidate verses: BG 18.73 (Arjuna says 'my doubt is destroyed'), BG 11.50 (Krishna shows his gentle form again to calm Arjuna), BG 4.7-8 (Krishna names his repeated incarnations to restore dharma). None of these turn on a settled-teacher-about-to-speak posture. The analog is anchored on the unique geometry of the BG 2 chariot moment + the iva-smile + the structural-pivot-before-teaching, all of which are 2.10-specific. PASS — the analog is not transferable.

verdict_payload.real_insight: Recognize the structural pivot moment — the room's readiness-to-receive-different-intervention — as a marker, not as a tactic; performing the affect (the leader's smile) without the underlying settled state inverts the verse.
verdict_payload.objection: I went looking for the 'smile before delivering hard feedback' wisdom-theater move and the verse explicitly refuses it: 'A reader extracts a leadership tactic from this verse — always smile before delivering hard feedback — they have over-read it.' The counter-example explicitly disqualifies the affect-performance read: 'A leader who performs a smile to manipulate the room is not enacting Krishna in 2.10; they are performing affect. The verse's smile is the byproduct of an actual settled state — not a technique that produces one.' The verse is honestly tagged LOW + STRETCHED and does not pretend to be a doctrine. The meta-discipline (don't read structural pivots as tactics) is real and engineering-relevant. wisdom_theater_score=0: this is the explicit refusal of wisdom theater.

verdict_payload.verdict_reason: BG 2.10 is narrative; no soteriologically load-bearing technical term is in play. The engineering layer correctly preserves the iva (as if) qualifier on prahasann — 'smiling \"as if\" — not amused, but settled' — which is the doctrinally important particle that all major commentators read carefully. No tradition is conflated; the engineering layer takes the cross-traditional consensus reading (the smile is settled-teacher's readiness, not amusement) and stays inside it. The geometry of the verse (between the two armies) is preserved without overload of doctrinal commitment. PASS.

verdict_payload.reconstructed_sketch: Given chapter context (2.6-2.9 are the case-closing arc, 2.11 will begin Krishna's reply), an engineering analog at position 10 would predictably be a transitional verse — possibly describing the room's geometry before the teaching, possibly a final pre-teaching beat. A competent writer might write 'recognize when the room is ready' — but they would not necessarily land on the precise refusal-to-extract-tactic move plus the iva-smile-preservation. The honest LOW + STRETCHED tag is itself information.
verdict_payload.verse_specific_signal: The iva-smile-preservation (the explicit naming of 'as if' as the qualifier that distinguishes settled-readiness from amusement) plus the explicit chapter-architecture identification: 'this verse merely names the pivot moment between the operator's exhausted silence and the teacher's settled reply.' This pivot-naming is verse-specific.

overlap_with_other_verses.explanation: Both 2.9 and 2.10 are scaffolding-pivot verses, both tagged LOW + STRETCHED. The pair-overlap is the chapter's sanctioned form for the structural pivot — 2.9 closes Arjuna's case, 2.10 opens Krishna's reply. The two verses are intentionally complementary, not duplicate.

verdict_payload.verdict_reason: The reconstructibility score is moderate (5). The chapter context licenses a transition-verse but does not predict the specific iva-preservation or the 'recognize structural pivots' meta-discipline contribution. The 2.9/2.10 pair-overlap is sanctioned by the chapter's pivot-pair structure. PASS.

verdict_payload.reproducible_artifact: Engineering-management coaching-curriculum predicate: 'Distinguish state-from-affect. Do not include any module that teaches affect-performance (smile/posture/tone) as a tactic for handling difficult conversations. Coaching content must address the underlying state — preparation, settled judgement, reframing-content — not the surface affect that is its byproduct.'
verdict_payload.embodiment_sketch: Add a vetting predicate to the engineering-management training curriculum-review template: 'Reject any module whose mechanism is affect-without-state.' Concrete check: 'Does this module teach a leader to smile/pause/lower-voice as a technique that produces the desired room-state?' If yes, reject — the verse explicitly disqualifies this read. The vetting predicate becomes a one-line rule on the L&D content-review checklist. Concretely copyable Monday.
verdict_payload.verdict_reason: The verse honestly tagged LOW + STRETCHED still licenses a real coaching-curriculum vetting predicate — distinguish state-driven affect from performed affect. This is a real review-discipline that L&D can apply to vetted training content. Not metaphor-only; concretely embodiable as a checklist line. PASS — though the artifact is meta (a vetting rule for curriculum), it is concretely copyable.

verdict_payload.notes: BG 2.10 is pure narration (Sanjaya describing Krishna's settled-teacher posture before he begins to speak at 2.11). The doctrinally significant element — prahasann iva (smiling 'as if'), where the iva qualifies the smile and distinguishes it from amusement — is preserved precisely by the engineering layer: 'smiling \"as if\" — not amused, but settled.' This honors the commentarial reading (Shankara explicitly notes the iva is meaningful — 'not a smile of amusement, a smile of teacher's settled readiness'). The geometry of the moment (senayor ubhayor madhye — between the two armies) is preserved in the analog ('the two armies of the room — the team that wanted the migration, the team that opposed it — are still arranged on either side of the issue'). The engineering layer correctly identifies the verse's role as the structural pivot before the teaching at 2.11 ('this verse merely names the pivot moment between the operator's exhausted silence and the teacher's settled reply') — which mirrors the commentarial consensus. The verse does not invert any Sanskrit claim; it correctly refuses to manufacture a teaching from the smile. PASS.

verdict_payload.actionable_predicate: When you observe a structural pivot moment in a meeting (the room transitioning from one mode to another), do not extract a tactic from the marker (e.g. 'smile-before-hard-feedback'); the meaningful content begins after the pivot, not at it.
verdict_payload.example_decision: In a contested architecture review where the room has just gone quiet after a hard 'I'm not going to lead this' declaration, the senior in the room should NOT prepare 'a coaching technique to deliver feedback warmly' — that is performing affect, which inverts the verse. They should prepare the actual reframing content (the thing they will say next), since the room's readiness is the precondition for that content, not for the affect. PM-side: do not put 'leader smile/posture coaching' on the engineering-management training calendar; do put 'reframing-content preparation' on it.
verdict_payload.objection: Caveat (logged as PASS): the predicate is meta-disciplinary rather than directly roadmap-facing. The verse is honestly tagged LOW + STRETCHED. PASS because the meta-discipline (don't extract tactics from structural pivots) is itself a real PM training-and-coaching discipline.

verdict_payload.verdict_reason: The verse is honestly tagged LOW + STRETCHED. The engineering layer treats Krishna's smile with the seriousness it deserves: it preserves the iva qualifier, refuses to extract a 'smile-before-feedback' tactic, and explicitly disclaims the affect-performance read. A serious practitioner would feel the verse has been honored by being correctly identified as the pre-teaching pivot rather than being trivialized into a coaching technique. The implication 'recognise structural pivots when you encounter them' is meta-disciplined and respectful of the source. Zero trivialization. PASS.

verdict_payload.chapter_voice_summary: BG 2.10 is in narrative-scaffolding register, appropriately distinct from the chapter's didactic voice but still in the chapter's grave register. The engineering layer holds the chapter's tone: it does not slip into corporate-coaching warmth (the obvious risk for a smile-verse) and explicitly refuses to read the smile as a leadership tactic. The framing 'production carries on entirely indifferent to anyone's pause' (carrying over from 2.9 narrative voice) and 'the room's readiness to receive a different kind of intervention' is in-tone with the chapter's senior-teacher-not-consoling register. PASS.
